data.body:
## Description

**What**: Implement the nava-platform CLI install path for templates that use copier-based installation.
**Why**: 3 of 4 templates (NextJS, Flask, Rails) require the nava-platform CLI. This story handles the full chain: check for package manager, install CLI, verify, run template install.
**Scope**:
- IN: Probe for uv then pipx on PATH. If neither found, print actionable install instructions and abort template path. Attempt CLI install via uv/pipx from GitHub. Verify nava-platform --help exits 0 (smoke test). Run nava-platform app install with properly quoted --data flags from registry. Timeout on all subprocess calls. Clear error messages at each failure point with manual fallback offer.
- OUT: Jekyll git clone path (story ab8a-8a49). Post-install onboarding flow (story da98-0163). Registry parsing (story bc4e-ec68).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, onboarding probes for uv then pipx and, if found, installs nava-platform CLI and verifies it is functional via --help exit code
  ← Satisfies: "probe uv/pipx, install CLI, verify --help exits 0"
- When this story is complete, nava-platform app install runs with all required --data flags from the registry, properly quoted, producing a fully non-interactive installation
  ← Satisfies: "run app install with properly quoted --data flags from registry"
- When this story is complete, if uv/pipx is not found OR CLI install fails OR app install fails, onboarding surfaces a clear error message and offers the manual flow as fallback
  ← Satisfies: "Any failure -> clear error + manual fallback"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Security] --data flag values must be properly quoted to prevent shell injection / word splitting.
- [Security] Install target must be restricted to CWD — validate no path traversal (../).
- [Reliability] All subprocess calls need timeouts to prevent hangs on slow/throttled connections.
- [Reliability] nava-platform may have a first-time config wizard — test with fresh install.
- [Reliability] Installed from GitHub HEAD — non-reproducible. Log exact commit installed.
- [Performance] GitHub clone + pip install can be slow — consider progress indication to user.
